Tycho Brahe's father is Otte Brahe; however, Tycho Brahe is considered the son of observational astronomy. 
The father of Tycho Brahe would be his biological parent but also more accurately described by being the grandfather of observational astronomy since he had an influence on it indirectly via his child who became famous for contributions to the field.

Nicolaus Copernicus has often been credited with starting modern astronomical thought due to his heliocentric model. However, there were others before him such as Galen of Pergamon’s rival – Aristarchus of Samos who proposed a sun-centered universe around 250 BCE.
